case_title: DERON A KARTOON VS. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., A CORPORATION et al

docket_entries.description: ANSWER TO COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION BANC OF AMERICA INVESTMENT SERVICES, INC., A CORPORATION DELANEY, KYLE , AN INDIVIDUAL CONN, MICHAEL ,AN INDIVIDUAL
SEPERATE STATEMENT OF ITEMS IN DISPUTE IN OPPOSITION TO DEFTS MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER FILED BY PLAINTIFF KARTOON, DERON A
OPPOSITION TO DEFTS PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS FILED BY PLAINTIFF KARTOON, DERON A

LAW AND MOTION 301, MR. LANDY REQUESTED TO TAKE THIS MATTER OFF CALENDAR WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE ISSUE WAS ARGUED AND THE OBJECTION AS TO THE ADMISSIBILITY OF THE U-4 DOCUMENT WAS OVERRULED. THEREFORE, ARGUMENT ON DEFENDANTS BANK OF AMERICA, NA, BANK OF AMERICA CORPORATION, BANC OF AMERICA INVESTMENT SERVICES, INC., KYLE DELANEY AND MICHAEL CONN'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS PENDING ARBITRATION WAS HEARD. THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: ARBITRATION IS COMPELLED. THE ARBITRATION OBLIGATION BASED ON U-4 IS PROPERLY IN EVIDENCE, AT LEAST AS TO BAI. AS THE COMPLAINT AND PLEADINGS ARE THE SAME AGAINST ALL DEFENDANTS IT IS APPROPRIATE TO ORDER ARBITRATION AS TO ALL DEFENDANTS ON THOSE CLAIMS. AS TO THE STAY, IT IS GRANTED EXCEPT THAT ALREADY OUTSTANDING WRITTEN DISCOVERY PROCEEDINGS TO COMPLETE DISCOVERY MAY PROCEED TO CONCLUSION. THIS RELATES TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY ALREADY PROPOUNDED AND DOES NOT INCLUDE DEPOSITION NOTICES JUST SENT. IN ADDITION, TO CONCLUDE THE PROCESS OF OBTAINING THE BENEFITS OF THE JULY 18, RULING FROM COMMISSIONER HEWLITT. PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING PREPARING A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR 8189

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And Verifications; Request For Judicial Notice; Declaration Of Elizabeth D. Rhodes (Vol 1+2)
Off Calendar Sep-17-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:


Appearance:
Harvey T. Elam, Esq., for Deron Kartoon, Plaintiff/Moving Party
Shannon N. Nakabayashi, Esq., for Bank of America, Defendant/Opposing Party
Mark I. Brickman, CSR #5527

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted with modifications: Plaintiff Deron Kartoon's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And Verifications is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as the objections of vague and ambiguous are overruled and ordered stricken. The responses otherwise are adequate. The motion is denied as it seeks further responses. The motion is moot as verifications are sought from Defendants Bank of America, N.A., Bank Of America Corporation, Banc of America Investment Services, Inc., and Michael Conn. The motion is granted as it seeks a verification from Defendant Kyle Delaney. His verification shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied.

Modification: Michael Conn to verify his responses. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Ntc Of Hearing On Defts Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Arbitration
Off Calendar Aug-07-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Harvey T. Elam of Kenney & Markowitz, LLP, 397-3100, appearing for plaintiff and responding party
Daryl S. Landy of Morgan Lewis, 650-843-7561, appearing for defendants and moving parties

Mr. Landy requested to take this matter off calendar without prejudice. The issue was argued and the objection as to the admissibility of the U-4 document was overruled. Therefore, argument on DEFENDANTS BANK OF AMERICA, NA, BANK OF AMERICA CORPORATION, BANC OF AMERICA INVESTMENT SERVICES, INC., KYLE DELANEY and MICHAEL CONN'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Arbitration was heard. The Court ruled as follows: Arbitration is compelled. The arbitration obligation based on U-4 is properly in evidence, at least as to BAI. As the complaint and pleadings are the same against all defendants it is appropriate to order arbitration as to all defendants on those claims. As to the stay, it is granted except that already outstanding written discovery proceedings to complete discovery may proceed to conclusion. This relates to written discovery already propounded and does not include deposition notices just sent. In addition, to conclude the process of obtaining the benefits of the July 18, ruling from Commissioner Hewlitt. Parties are to meet and confer regarding preparing a form of order. (301/PJB/PM) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189
